perturbation.original_text: Legal Remedy ------------ You understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by your or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity provided however any suit for damages shall be specifically limited to actual provable damages, and Company hereby waives any and all rights to seek consequential, punitive, treble or other compensatory damages of any types or kind.
perturbation.changed_text: Other ----- This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto or their authorized representatives. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to the conflicts of law principles thereof. Legal Remedy ------------ You understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by your or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity provided however any suit for damages shall be specifically limited to actual provable damages, and Company hereby waives any and all rights to seek consequential, punitive, treble or other compensatory damages of any types or kind.
perturbation.explanation: The legal remedy clause is relocated to the 'Other' section, making it less prominent and harder to find for a party reviewing the contract. This obscures the limitations on damages, which could be considered a violation of good faith and fair dealing in contract law.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code Section 1668 states that contracts that exempt anyone from responsibility for their own fraud, willful injury, or violation of law are against public policy. Placing the limitation of damages within the 'Other' section buries it, potentially obscuring a waiver of rights to certain damages that could arise from such actions. The location affects its visibility and could be challenged as an attempt to obscure liability for potentially fraudulent or unlawful behavior.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1668. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 4. UNLAWFUL CONTRACTS [1667 - 1670.12] ( Title 4 enacted 1872. ) 1668. All contracts which have for their object, directly or indirectly, to exempt any one from responsibility for his own fraud, or willful injury to the person or property of another, or violation of law, whether willful or negligent, are against the policy of the law. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modified contract relocates the legal remedy clause, including limitations on damages, to the 'Other' section. This structural change obscures the limitations, making them less visible to a reviewing party. California Civil Code Section 1668 states that contracts designed to exempt someone from responsibility for their own fraud, willful injury, or violation of law are against public policy. By burying the limitation of damages, the contract could be interpreted as attempting to shield the company from liability for potentially fraudulent or unlawful behavior, which contradicts public policy as outlined in the law snippet.

perturbation.original_text: No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation --------------------------------------- During the course of your evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to employees or representatives of the Company as specified by the Company. You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party that involves use of the Evaluation Materials without the Company's prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: No Representation or Warranty ----------------------------- No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation --------------------------------------- During the course of your evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to employees or representatives of the Company as specified by the Company. You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party that involves use of the Evaluation Materials without the Company's prior written consent. Although the Company has endeavored to include in the Evaluation Materials information which it believes to be relevant for the purpose of your investigation, you acknowledge and agree that none of the Company or the Company's representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and none of the Company or the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to you or any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any of your representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By embedding the 'No Unauthorized Contact' clause within the 'No Representation or Warranty' section, it creates an implication that the restriction on contact is somehow related to the accuracy of the information provided. This might suggest that the limitation on contact only applies if the company warrants the information's accuracy.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code § 17200 prohibits unfair competition. While not directly related, misleadingly placing the 'No Unauthorized Contact' clause could be considered an unfair business practice if it deceives the other party into thinking the contact restriction is contingent upon the warranty of information, potentially stifling their ability to verify the information from other sources. An entity may be disadvantaged by being restricted to verify the information of the evaluation materials if its ability to discuss with other parties, which is now located in 'No Representation or Warranty,' is made unclear.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 17200 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 5. Enforcement [17200 - 17210] ( Chapter 5 added by Stats. 1977, Ch. 299. ) 17200. As used in this chapter, unfair competition shall mean and include any unlawful, unfair or fraudulent business act or practice and unfair, deceptive, untrue or misleading advertising and any act prohibited by Chapter 1 (commencing with Section 17500) of Part 3 of Division 7 of the Business and Professions Code. (Amended by Stats. 1992, Ch. 430, Sec. 2. Effective January 1, 1993.)

perturbation.justification: The structural change introduces a legal contradiction. The original text presents a clear restriction against unauthorized contact. The changed text embeds this restriction within a 'No Representation or Warranty' section. This placement creates an ambiguity, suggesting the contact restriction is contingent on the accuracy of the information. According to the Law Explanation, this misplacement could be an unfair business practice under California Business and Professions Code § 17200, because it can deceive the other party into thinking that the contact restriction is contingent upon the warranty of information. Scraped Law Snippet 1 and Scraped Law Snippet 2 both state that Section 17200 defines unfair competition as including any 'unfair or fraudulent business act or practice'. By potentially misleading the counterparty about the scope of the contact restriction, the structural flaw in the contract could be viewed as an unfair business practice.

perturbation.original_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). You recognize and acknowledge the confidential and non-public nature of the Evaluation Materials, the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials, and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection regarding the Company, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential". The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other
perturbation.changed_text: Return of Documents ------------------- You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). You recognize and acknowledge the confidential and non-public nature of the Evaluation Materials, the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials, and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection regarding the Company, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential". The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other. Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Placing the 'Return of Documents' wording after the definition of Evaluation Materials makes the 'Return of Documents' section seem as if it’s just clarifying what happens to Evaluation Materials. This could lead to the reader not understanding the importance of returning the documents, as it is located within a definition.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Evidence Code Section 623 addresses estoppel based on a party's own statement or conduct. Moving the 'Return of Documents' obligation into the middle of the 'Confidentiality' section and altering the heading could lead a party to reasonably believe the return obligation is only applicable under certain conditions of confidentiality. This could lead to the company claiming the other party is estopped from denying the return of documents, because they have already agreed to do so, but their actual agreement is obscure due to its location.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, EVID 623 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Evidence Code - EVID DIVISION 5. BURDEN OF PROOF; BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE; PRESUMPTIONS AND INFERENCES [500 - 670] ( Division 5 enacted by Stats. 1965, Ch. 299. ) CHAPTER 3. Presumptions and Inferences [600 - 670] ( Chapter 3 enacted by Stats. 1965, Ch. 299. ) ARTICLE 2. Conclusive Presumptions [620 - 624] ( Article 2 enacted by Stats. 1965, Ch. 299. ) 623. Whenever a party has, by his own statement or conduct, intentionally and deliberately led another to believe a particular thing true and to act upon such belief, he is not, in any litigation arising out of such statement or conduct, permitted to contradict it. (Enacted by Stats. 1965, Ch. 299.)
perturbation.justification: The modification to the contract creates a structural flaw by placing the 'Return of Documents' clause within the definition of 'Evaluation Materials', which is misleading. The LLM explanation states that this "could lead a party to reasonably believe the return obligation is only applicable under certain conditions of confidentiality", weakening the enforceability of the return obligation. California Evidence Code Section 623 states that a party is estopped from contradicting a belief they intentionally led another to act upon. By obscuring the return obligation, the modified contract introduces ambiguity around this action. This obscurity could allow a party to argue they were not fully aware of the return obligation, potentially contradicting the company's reliance on their agreement to return documents, thus creating a contradiction as the placement affects clarity and enforceability.
